evaluation.manager_summary: William handled a cost-anxious caller (Stephanie) seeking help for daily meth use with excellent objection handling and transparency, immediately offering three financial alternatives and a free financial counselor consult. He captured insurance details, substance use history, and withdrawal symptoms appropriately. Main coaching opportunity: next-step clarity was initially vague ('we'll be in touch') until the caller pressed for specifics—he should lead with timing and ownership upfront.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent immediately validated the caller's cost concern and acknowledged the center's own transparency shortcoming. Tone was conversational and non-scripted, building trust quickly. Minor opportunity: could have used caller's name (Stephanie) after she introduced herself.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Use the caller's name after they introduce themselves to deepen rapport.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent collected carrier (BluePeak Health), member ID (BP77860047), and group number (GRP-979152). Importantly, framed coverage discussion as transparent rather than gatekeeping, and immediately pivoted to three concrete alternatives when high deductible emerged. Minor gap: plan type not explicitly captured.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Ask for plan type (PPO, HMO, EPO) in addition to carrier, member ID, and group number.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent captured substance type (meth), duration (eighteen months), frequency (daily), and withdrawal symptoms (exhaustion, depression). Stayed in intake role with appropriate boundary-setting language. Minor gap: did not ask about prior treatment history or co-occurring mental health concerns.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Ask about prior treatment attempts and whether any mental health concerns are present alongside substance use.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent correctly identified daily meth use and withdrawal symptoms but did not explicitly probe for higher-urgency signals (recent overdose, suicidal ideation, unsafe living situation). No manufacturing of urgency occurred. Appropriate level of response for information presented.
Caller is the patient themselves, confirmed by first-person language throughout.

Agent immediately acknowledged the cost concern before responding, validated anxiety, and offered three concrete alternatives with specifics (sliding scale, CareCredit 0% for 12 months, state funds). Explained complexity honestly without being defensive. Offered free, no-obligation financial counselor consult to address the objection directly.

Agent committed to same-day callback from financial team but used vague language initially ('We'll be in touch'). Only after caller explicitly asked 'When?' and then 'Today?' did agent confirm same-day timing. Ownership unclear (who specifically will call). No mention of what caller should prepare or what happens after financial consult.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Lead with specific timing and ownership upfront: 'Our financial counselor [name] will call you today by [time]. They'll need [documents]. After that call, we'll schedule your clinical intake if you decide to move forward.'

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent stayed firmly within intake boundaries. Used appropriate qualifying language ('Our clinical team will assess level of care') and educational framing ('That's consistent with stimulant withdrawal') without diagnosing. No outcome guarantees. No unauthorized practice of medicine.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: That's a completely valid concern and I'm glad you called to ask rather than just not calling.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Cost transparency is something we should be better at.

evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Let me be honest about that rather than oversell it.

evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Yes, BluePeak Health. But I have a high deductible. Member ID BP77860047, group GRP-979152.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: We have a sliding scale based on income... CareCredit — 0% for twelve months... state substance use treatment funds that can cover the gap entirely.

evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Let me be honest about that rather than oversell it.

evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Our clinical team will assess level of care, but for daily meth use at eighteen months, IOP is often the starting point.

evidence_anchors.text_snippet: We have a sliding scale... CareCredit — 0% for twelve months... state substance use treatment funds that can cover the gap entirely.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Our financial counsellor can run through all three with you in about fifteen minutes and give you actual numbers. That call is free and there's no obligation.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Yes, today. Keep your phone handy.

evidence_anchors.text_snippet: That's consistent with stimulant withdrawal — it's real even if it's not physically dangerous the way opioid or alcohol withdrawal can be.
